# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Rocket League...
- Steam AppID of the game: 252950...

## System Information
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 470.57.02
- Kernel version: 5.11.0-27-generic (ubuntu 20.04)
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/cassbeck/14d730973ba48d7e8262c5f7d2fac53c
- Proton version: 6.3-6 

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-252950.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7025945/steam-252950.log)
https://gist.github.com/cassbeck/786b362f30a0870f962c7eeb23062d9c


## Symptoms 
- program does not start, errors are in the logs `Could not connect to X session manager: Authentication Rejected, reason : MIT-MAGIC-COOKIE-1 authentication rejected`

- each time I try to start Rocket League with Proton 6.3-6
- Rocket League is working with Proton 5.13-6 but it is very slow

Hello @alexzk1, since you're symlinking to an unexpected folder, you might be seeing an issue where the symlink destination isn't getting mounted into the Steam Linux Runtime - Soldier container environment. Please give https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-runtime/issues/334 a read (at least the first handful of comments).

If that's not part of your issue, then I'd expect setting STEAM_COMPAT_MOUNTS wouldn't have a significant effect.

Leaving a comment here even though this issue has been closed for a while, since it's referenced from elsewhere when talking about using non-default directories with Proton and the Steam Linux Runtime:

Starting from pressure-vessel version 0.20220803.0 in today's "Steam Linux Runtime - soldier" and "Steam Linux Runtime - sniper" betas, more top-level directories are available to games by default, reducing the need to use `STEAM_COMPAT_MOUNTS`. Please see ValveSoftware/steam-runtime/issues/470 for more details.

Of the paths mentioned here, the FHS paths `/srv/steam` and `/mnt/applications` no longer need `STEAM_COMPAT_MOUNTS` with this change, but the custom/non-standard paths `/ssdgames` and `/storage` still would.

## Symptoms
Videos in story mode don't play, only the intro.

## Reproduction
There are two ways:
- Start Calamity Trigger story mode. The video does not play.
- Go to the gallery section and try playing a video.

Here is what happens:
![20230713180436_1](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/11358308/03172d6c-3a1b-43a7-9d51-816fe6b6f8f6)

## Debug
What I found so far:
- The videos can be found in `data/Movie` of the installation directory.
- The sound codec is `wmav2` and the video codec is `wmv3` for all videos, so gstreamer needs the libav plugin.
- The intro video plays correctly, but gallery videos don't play at all, despite having the same codecs.
- All videos play with ProtonGE8-6, but GE the sound does not work.
- Proton includes gstreamer libraries so it's not related to the OS.
- There's some kind of registry change in `compatdata/294810/gstreamer-1.0`.
- BlazBlue: Calamity Trigger didn't seem to have the same problem.
- Found out not all story mode videos fail, only some.

Download SSB from https://supreme-snowboarding.eu/. It is a limited edition which is shareware but that already doesn't work so I don't think the full version does either. The full version is harder to come by.

Copy the installer to the Steam Deck using an SD card. Run Steam on the SteamDeck in Desktop Mode and add the installer as a new game. Find the installed game files in `home/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/meaninglessnumber/pfx/c_drive/Games/Supreme` and add it as a non-Steam game to Steam in Desktop Mode again.

Switch to Gaming Mode, set compatibility to Proton Experimental and attempt to run the game. It will crash right on startup.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
If anyone stumbles over this high-rated search engine result. I am the maintainer of the Lutris installer for Supreme Snowboard, created in collaboration with the developer and maintainer of the Supreme Snowboard Limited Edition.

Some notes: Don't install your games into the Proton prefix (`compatdata/meaninglessnumber`). It will be replaced or modified by Steam. Changing your Proton version will give you a new prefix with the game missing. Use some folder outside of Steam (for example `/home/deck/Games`).

I can successfully play Supreme Snowboard on my Steamdeck when installing it through Lutris. But as the gamepad support and resolution selection of Supreme Snowboarding is weak, I don't bother playing it from Game Mode and instead play it from desktop, in a window, with Software graphics.

If you want to try it or just see what the installer does, so you can replicate it with Proton, check the Lutris game page and the installer here: https://lutris.net/games/supreme-snowboarding/

Cheers!
